title: osm_cloud crashes when started with a GPX file
desc: OSMCloud.__init__ calls self.map_data.run_all(self.save_mapdata), but MapData.run_all declares save as keyword-only (def run_all(self, *, save=True)), so the call raises TypeError: run_all() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given. With respawn=True in the launch file this becomes a crash loop. Fix: run_all(save=self.save_mapdata).

title: Custom highway/surface costs from the viewer UI are silently ignored
desc: ReplanPath.__init__ passes self.HIGHWAY_COSTS/self.SURFACE_COSTS into PathGrid at construction time. The viewer routes (get_cost_grid, create_replan) construct the ReplanPath first and then assign replanner.HIGHWAY_COSTS = custom_costs — this rebinds an instance attribute that the already-built PathGrid never sees, so the defaults are always used. The planner UI exposes and sends these costs, so user edits have no effect. Fix: accept the costs as ReplanPath constructor arguments and pass them through to PathGrid. While at it, remove the dead try/except (ValueError, TypeError) around the plain attribute assignments in create_replan — assignment cannot raise those.

title: Refreshing OSM data on a loaded .mapdata crashes
desc: load_mapdata never restores BARRIER_TAGS/OBSTACLE_TAGS (and friends), _obstacle_radius, or _buffer_widths, so MapData.load(...) followed by run_queries() + run_parse() raises AttributeError. Fix: call md._load_tag_configs() and default the two private fields to None in load_mapdata.

title: GPX mode is unreachable from the launch file
desc: The launch file always passes PathJoinSubstitution([mapdata_path, mapdata_file]), which produces a non-empty string, so the node's if self.mapdata_file is not None branch is always taken and the gpx_file argument is dead. If the default stromovka.mapdata is missing (it is gitignored, and setup.py only installs *.gpx into the share dir), MapData.load raises and respawn=True turns it into a crash loop. Fix: use an empty-string sentinel for unset file arguments and treat "" as None in the node.

title: Cost grids contain phantom zero-cost cells
desc: PathGrid.create_empty_grid generates points with np.linspace(low, high, ceil(range/cell_size)), whose spacing is slightly larger than cell_size, while get_grid_2d maps points back with floor((x − low) / cell_size). The mismatch means some 2-D cells are never written and keep their np.zeros initial value — cost 0.0, i.e. maximally attractive to the planners — while the last row/column collides via clipping. The same linspace pattern exists in osm_cloud.create_grid. Fix: generate points with np.arange(low, high, cell_size) (or num+1 linspace points) and initialize grid_2d to default_off_path_cost instead of zeros so unwritten cells are pessimistic, not free.

title: Annotation store writes are non-atomic and unguarded
desc: save_annotations rewrites the JSON file in place — a crash mid-write corrupts it, and two concurrent requests doing read-modify-write lose each other's updates (Flask serves requests on multiple threads). load_annotations has no JSONDecodeError handling, so one corrupted file then 500s every endpoint for that mapdata. Fix: write to a temp file + os.replace, add a per-file lock, and load tolerantly.

title: Stale __version__ in package __init__
desc: map_data/__init__.py still says 1.0.0 while pyproject.toml, package.xml, and CITATION.cff say 1.1.0. The 1.1.0 changelog even claims stale version numbers were fixed. The Overpass client User-Agent also hardcodes map_data/1.1. Consider single-sourcing via importlib.metadata.version("map_data") so it cannot drift again.

title: grid_topic launch argument is declared but never used
desc: The launch file declares a grid_topic argument (default osm_grid) but never passes it to the node parameters, so it silently does nothing and the node uses the YAML value (grid) instead. Either wire it into the node parameters or drop the argument.

title: parameter_callback creates intersection publishers on hardcoded topics
desc: When publish_intersections is enabled at runtime via the parameter callback, the publishers are created on hardcoded "intersections"/"intersection_markers" instead of the configured self.intersections_topic/self.intersection_markers_topic used at init time.

title: Quadratic neighbor cost is double-normalized
desc: points_near_ref already returns dist / max_path_dist (normalized to [0, 1)). The "quadratic" branch in get_cloud squares it — still normalized — and then divides by max_path_dist**2 again. Correct only when max_path_dist == 1.0 (the default); any other value silently squashes the cost gradient. Fix: drop the extra division.

title: TrackerNode.num_waypoints is never updated
desc: num_waypoints is initialized to 0 and never set from any subscription, but the NavigateThroughPoses feedback handler computes current_waypoint = num_waypoints - number_of_poses_remaining, which goes negative for that action type. Update num_waypoints when a path/goal is received, or derive the index differently.

title: Way.to_pcd_points ignores its density parameter for linestrings
desc: The non-polygon branch hardcodes get_point_line(p1, p2, 0.5), so the documented "points per metre" knob only affects filled polygons. Note the semantics differ: density is points per metre for the grid fill, while get_point_line takes a step size — the linestring branch should pass 1 / density.

title: Way.to_pcd_points cache ignores its arguments
desc: The first call caches pcd_points; any later call with a different density or filled silently returns the stale cached cloud. Either key the cache on the arguments or drop the caching.

title: Polygon holes are re-blocked when rasterizing obstacles
desc: PathGrid.fill subtracts path geometries from obstacles so footways crossing a barrier stay traversable, but both the point-grid masking and burn_obstacles rasterize with Path(obstacle.exterior.coords) only — interior holes are ignored. A path that punches a hole through a polygon (rather than splitting it) gets blocked again anyway. Fix: subtract hole masks built from polygon.interiors, or rasterize with Shapely contains on prepared geometries.

title: Inconsistent UTM zone forcing during parsing
desc: MapData.get_points() forces the instance zone via force_zone_number/force_zone_letter, but parse_osm_ways, parse_osm_nodes, and parse_intersections call utm.from_latlon without forcing. Near a UTM zone boundary, way geometry and node coordinates can land in a different zone than the metadata claims (a warning is logged, but geometry is still inconsistent). Force the zone everywhere for consistency.

title: combine_ways crashes when linemerge yields a MultiLineString
desc: When relation members do not connect end-to-end (gaps, duplicate segments), linemerge(current_lines) returns a MultiLineString, and the subsequent geometry.Polygon(merged_line.coords) raises NotImplementedError mid-parse. Add a geom-type guard with a fallback (e.g. keep the member ways unmerged).

title: upload_gpx converts intended 503/500 responses into generic 500s
desc: abort(503, "Overpass API unavailable") and abort(500, "Parsing failed") raise HTTPException, which the surrounding except Exception as e: abort(500, str(e)) catches — the client never sees the intended status, and internal exception text leaks into responses. Fix: re-raise HTTPException before the generic handler.

title: parse_gpx_file returns an inconsistent shape on empty input
desc: On a GPX with no waypoints, parse_gpx_file/parse_yaml_file return (np.array([]), None, None) instead of the documented [], so the replan CLI proceeds and builds a MapData with zone_number=None, failing far from the cause. Return a consistent empty value and fail fast.

title: fetch_area task status pops on first poll; unpolled tasks leak
desc: fetch_area_status removes the task entry as soon as a terminal state is read once — a retried poll or a second tab gets a spurious 404. Tasks that are never polled stay in _fetch_tasks forever. Fix: keep terminal results for a grace period and expire entries by age.

title: get_cost_grid rejects zero-valued coordinates
desc: The parameter check uses if not all([filename, min_lat, min_lon, max_lat, max_lon]), so a legitimate 0.0 latitude or longitude (equator/prime meridian) aborts with 400. Use explicit is None checks.

title: RRTStar __main__ demo is broken
desc: The example passes plain tuples for start/goal; self.goal - self.start in __init__ raises TypeError on tuples. Convert to np.array in the demo (or in the constructor).

title: Harden the viewer for network deployment
desc: Defaults are sane (binds 127.0.0.1, path traversal guarded, pickle loading removed), but robot deployments will use --host 0.0.0.0: SocketIO allows cors_allowed_origins="*", no endpoint has authentication (arbitrary file creation in the data dir, spawning wormhole subprocesses, triggering Overpass fetches), and abort(500, str(e)) leaks exception details. Add a deployment-security docs section, tighten error messages, and consider an optional access token.

title: parse_gpx_file only reads waypoints, not tracks or routes
desc: utils/gpx.py only iterates gpx.waypoints, while MapData falls back to tracks and routes. A track-only GPX therefore works for map download but not as replan CLI path input. Reuse the same waypoints → tracks → routes fallback.

title: Split routes.py and deduplicate the way-resolution pipeline
desc: routes.py is ~1,800 lines mixing annotations, way editing, planning, and wormhole sharing. The way lookup + edit-application pipeline (deletions → added nodes → position overrides → split segments, plus synthetic-node cache construction) is triplicated nearly verbatim in get_way_nodes, get_way, and _get_way_segments_geojson. Extract a shared _resolve_way(filename, way_id, store) helper and split the blueprint into modules.

title: Shrink the get_cost_grid response
desc: The endpoint returns one GeoJSON Feature per grid point — hundreds of thousands of features for a modest bbox at 1 m resolution. A flat [[lat, lon, cost], ...] array (or a rendered PNG overlay + bounds) would cut the payload roughly 10×.

title: Filter Overpass queries by tag families
desc: run_queries downloads every way and node in the bbox (way(bbox), node(bbox)). Filtering server-side by the tag families the parser actually uses (highway, barrier, building, natural, landuse, …) would shrink downloads and parse time substantially.

title: Add type checking and pre-commit hooks
desc: The code is fully annotated but no checker runs — mypy/pyright in CI would have caught the keyword-only run_all crash and the Response return-type inconsistencies. Ruff is configured but there is no pre-commit config to run it locally.

title: Hardcoded fallback costs drift from planner_defaults.yaml
desc: The fallback dicts in ReplanPath (used only when the config file is missing) have sand: 0.7 while config/planner_defaults.yaml says sand: 0.4. Sync the values, or drop the duplicated fallback dicts and treat the YAML as the single source of truth.

title: Speed up planning hot paths with native extensions
desc: The main bottlenecks are the Python _bresenham generator and _segment_cost in RRT* (called O(max_iter × neighbors) times), and the heapq loop in grid_astar. Recommended approach: (1) try @numba.njit on these inner loops first — zero build overhead, easy to toggle; (2) replace grid_astar with skimage.graph.route_through_array which is C-backed; (3) Cython or pybind11 only if numba is unacceptable for deployment. Full C/C++ rewrite is not justified given that numpy, cKDTree, and Shapely/GEOS are already native.

title: Remove no-op threading parallelism in replan
desc: ReplanPath.replan uses Parallel(n_jobs=-1, backend="threading") over pure-Python A* — GIL-bound, so near-zero speedup — and threads can race to build grid_2d_cache (redundant work, benign result). Either switch to a process backend or drop the parallelism; the native-extension item is the better lever.

title: Unify shallow vs deep copy of the cached MapData
desc: get_mapdata shallow-copies the cached MapData (copy.copy) while get_merged_mapdata deep-copies. Correctness currently relies on every helper carefully copy.copy-ing ways before mutating them — a fragile invariant. Either document it prominently or make both paths consistent.

title: Elevation-aware planning costs
desc: Integrate DEM tiles and add a slope penalty term in PathGrid.fill so the planners avoid steep terrain, not just tagged obstacles.

title: Viewer undo/redo built on the existing change_log
desc: The annotation store already records ordered, timestamped change entries; exposing undo/redo in the UI would mostly be frontend work plus inverse operations for each change type.

title: Progress reporting for fetch_area via SocketIO
desc: The fetch task is polled over REST while a SocketIO channel already exists for telemetry — pushing progress (query, parse, save stages) would remove the polling and its 404-on-second-poll footgun.

title: GeoJSON export alongside .mapdata
desc: The viewer already converts merged map data to GeoJSON internally; exposing it as an export format would make the data usable in QGIS/geojson.io without custom tooling.

title: Multi-robot support in the tracker
desc: The robot_id parameter suggests this is anticipated; supporting several TrackerNode namespaces and tagging telemetry per robot would allow one viewer to monitor a fleet.

title: Unit-test osm_cloud's pure functions and node init
desc: create_grid, points_near_ref, split_ways_to_points, and transform_points need no ROS and are untested — a test would have caught the quadratic-normalization bug. Add an rclpy-mocked node-init test to catch signature bugs like the keyword-only run_all crash.

title: Regression test: no zero-cost cells off-path in a filled grid
desc: A test asserting that no cell in a filled PathGrid retains cost 0.0 away from any way would catch the linspace/floor discretization mismatch and future regressions of it.

title: Edge-case tests for parsing and GPX helpers
desc: Missing coverage: combine_ways with disconnected relation members (MultiLineString path), to_pcd_points cache invalidation across argument changes, and the empty-GPX return shape of parse_gpx_file.

title: Launch file smoke test
desc: No test exercises osm_cloud.launch.py. A launch_testing smoke test — or at minimum an assertion that every declared launch argument is consumed — would catch dead arguments like grid_topic and the unreachable GPX mode.

title: Measure coverage in CI
desc: 140 tests pass but nothing reports what they touch. Add pytest-cov with a CI threshold to make coverage gaps (osm_cloud, grid_astar internals, viewer error paths) visible.

title: README/docs gaps: info CLI, viewer flags, API-key env vars
desc: The README omits map_data_info (stats + --validate) and the viewer's --data-dir/--host/--port flags; the docs never mention the THUNDERFOREST_API_KEY/SEZNAM_API_KEY environment variables the index page reads for tile layers.

title: Changelog has no entries for unreleased work
desc: docs/dev/changelog.md stops at 1.1.0; post-release fixes (e.g. the osm_cloud logger fixes) are undocumented. Keep an Unreleased section current as changes land.

title: Docstrings for grid_constructor, viewer helpers and routes
desc: NumPy-style docstrings are consistently good in map_data.py and rrt_star.py but absent in grid_constructor.py, viewer/helpers.py, and most of viewer/routes.py — precisely the modules with the subtlest behaviour (grid indexing, way-edit pipeline).
